question: [File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: repay()] Can an unprivileged borrower under a market whose loanToken silently deducts a transfer fee call repay(market, units, onBehalf, address(0), '') so that position[id][onBehalf].debt is reduced by units (line 508) and marketState[id].withdrawable is increased by units (line 509) while SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom only delivers units*(1-fee_rate) to address(this) (line 520), violating the invariant that withdrawable <= actual loanToken balance of Midnight, causing scoped impact: protocol insolvency where lenders collectively withdraw more loanToken than the contract holds? Proof idea: deploy a fee-on-transfer loanToken with 1% fee; fuzz units in [1, type(uint128).max]; after N repayments assert midnight.withdrawable(id) > loanToken.balanceOf(address(midnight)).

[File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: repay() + withdraw()] Can an unprivileged lender under a fee-on-transfer loanToken market call repay() N times to inflate marketState[id].withdrawable beyond the actual loanToken balance, then call withdraw() to drain the contract leaving later lenders unable to withdraw, violating the invariant that sum(credit_i) <= withdrawable <= loanToken.balanceOf(midnight), causing scoped impact: fund freeze for remaining lenders and protocol insolvency? Proof idea: stateful fuzz with two lenders and one borrower; borrower repays in N chunks with fee-on-transfer token; assert first lender can withdraw full credit but second lender's withdraw() reverts due to insufficient balance.

[File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: supplyCollateral()] Can an unprivileged borrower under a market whose collateralToken silently deducts a transfer fee call supplyCollateral(market, collateralIndex, assets, onBehalf) so that position[id][onBehalf].collateral[collateralIndex] is set to oldCollateral + assets (line 533) while SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom only delivers assets*(1-fee_rate) to address(this) (line 545), violating the invariant that position.collateral[i] <= collateralToken.balanceOf(midnight) for each collateral slot, causing scoped impact: borrower can borrow against phantom collateral and withdraw more collateral than the protocol holds? Proof idea: deploy fee-on-transfer collateralToken; supplyCollateral(assets=1e18); assert position.collateral > collateralToken.balanceOf(midnight); then withdrawCollateral(assets=1e18) succeeds while protocol balance goes negative.

[File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: supplyCollateral() + isHealthy() + take()] Can an unprivileged borrower under a fee-on-transfer collateralToken market supply overcounted collateral via supplyCollateral(), pass the isHealthy() check in take() using the inflated position.collateral[i] value (line 954), and borrow more loanToken than the actual collateral value supports, violating the invariant that debt <= sum(collateral_i * price_i * lltv_i / WAD) based on real token balances, causing scoped impact: undercollateralized borrowing and bad debt creation? Proof idea: fuzz fee_rate in [1, 50]%; supply collateral; take sell offer; assert actual collateral value * lltv < debt.

[File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: take() + claimableSettlementFee] Can an unprivileged taker under a fee-on-transfer loanToken market execute take() so that claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] is incremented by buyerAssets - sellerAssets (line 418) while SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) (line 455) delivers only (buyerAssets - sellerAssets)*(1-fee_rate), violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee[token] <= loanToken.balanceOf(midnight) minus withdrawable minus continuousFeeCredit, causing scoped impact: feeClaimer can drain more than the protocol holds, creating insolvency? Proof idea: deploy fee-on-transfer loanToken; execute N takes; assert claimableSettlementFee > actual balance surplus; claimSettlementFee() drains lender-withdrawable funds.

[File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: take() two safeTransferFrom calls] Can an unprivileged taker under a fee-on-transfer loanToken market trigger both safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) (line 455) and safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, receiver, sellerAssets) (line 456) where both calls lose fee_rate percent, so the protocol receives (buyerAssets - sellerAssets)*(1-fee_rate) instead of the full settlement fee while the seller's credit is reduced by sellerCreditDecrease units at face value, violating the invariant that loanToken.balanceOf(midnight) >= withdrawable + claimableSettlementFee + continuousFeeCredit, causing scoped impact: compounded insolvency from two simultaneous fee deductions per take? Proof idea: fuzz buyerAssets and sellerAssets; assert sum of actual received amounts < sum of accounting increments.

[File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: flashLoan()] Can an unprivileged flash loan caller under a fee-on-transfer token market call flashLoan([token], [assets], callback, data) so that safeTransfer(token, callback, assets) (line 743) sends assets but callback receives assets*(1-fee_rate), and then safeTransferFrom(token, callback, address(this), assets) (line 750) pulls assets from callback but protocol receives assets*(1-fee_rate), resulting in a net loss of assets*fee_rate per flash loan from the protocol's balance, violating the invariant that token balances are non-decreasing after flashLoan, causing scoped impact: repeated flash loans drain the protocol's token reserves? Proof idea: deploy fee-on-transfer token; call flashLoan in a loop; assert midnight.loanToken.balanceOf(midnight) decreases by assets*fee_rate per iteration.

[File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: liquidate() + safeTransferFrom loanToken] Can an unprivileged liquidator under a fee-on-transfer loanToken market call liquidate() so that marketState[id].withdrawable is increased by repaidUnits (line 675) and position[id][borrower].debt is decreased by repaidUnits (line 676) while SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), repaidUnits) (line 717) only delivers repaidUnits*(1-fee_rate), violating the invariant that withdrawable <= loanToken.balanceOf(midnight), causing scoped impact: each liquidation inflates withdrawable beyond actual balance, compounding insolvency with repay()? Proof idea: fuzz repaidUnits; liquidate N positions; assert withdrawable > loanToken.balanceOf(midnight).

[File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: withdraw() + fee-on-transfer loanToken outgoing] Can an unprivileged lender under a fee-on-transfer loanToken market call withdraw(market, units, onBehalf, receiver) so that position[id][onBehalf].credit is reduced by units (line 493) and marketState[id].withdrawable is reduced by units (line 494) while SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, units) (line 499) only delivers units*(1-fee_rate) to receiver, violating the invariant that receiver's balance increases by exactly units, causing scoped impact: lender loses fee_rate percent of their credit redemption with no protocol recourse? Proof idea: fuzz units; assert receiver.balanceOf after withdraw < units; assert credit correctly zeroed.

[File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: claimContinuousFee() + fee-on-transfer loanToken] Can an unprivileged feeClaimer under a fee-on-transfer loanToken market call claimContinuousFee(market, amount, receiver) so that marketState[id].continuousFeeCredit, totalUnits, and withdrawable are all reduced by amount (lines 318-320) while SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, amount) (line 324) only delivers amount*(1-fee_rate), violating the invariant that the reduction in withdrawable equals the actual tokens sent to receiver, causing scoped impact: lenders' withdrawable is reduced by more than the tokens actually leaving the protocol, creating a shortfall? Proof idea: deploy fee-on-transfer loanToken; accrue continuousFee; claimContinuousFee; assert withdrawable decreased by amount but receiver got amount*(1-fee_rate).

[File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: withdraw() + false-return loanToken] Can an unprivileged lender under a market whose loanToken starts returning false on transfer call withdraw(market, units, onBehalf, receiver) so that SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer (line 499) reverts with TransferReturnedFalse after position[id][onBehalf].credit and marketState[id].withdrawable have been decremented (lines 493-494), but since the whole tx reverts the state is rolled back, violating the liveness invariant that a lender with valid credit and sufficient withdrawable must always be able to redeem, causing scoped impact: permanent fund freeze for all lenders if loanToken's transfer returns false? [File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: withdrawCollateral() + false-return collateralToken] Can an unprivileged borrower under a market whose collateralToken starts returning false on transfer call withdrawCollateral(market, collateralIndex, assets, onBehalf, receiver) so that SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, assets) (line 572) reverts with TransferReturnedFalse after position[id][onBehalf].collateral[collateralIndex] has been decremented (line 562) and the health check has passed (line 568), but since the whole tx reverts the state is rolled back, violating the liveness invariant that a healthy borrower must always be able to withdraw collateral, causing scoped impact: permanent collateral freeze if collateralToken's transfer returns false? [File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: liquidate() + false-return collateralToken] Can an unprivileged liquidator under a market whose collateralToken returns false on transfer call liquidate() so that SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) (line 696) reverts with TransferReturnedFalse after all state updates (bad debt, collateral, withdrawable, debt) have been applied (lines 626-676) but the whole tx reverts rolling back state, violating the liveness invariant that unhealthy positions must always be liquidatable, causing scoped impact: permanent liquidation freeze if collateralToken's transfer returns false, leaving bad debt unrealizable? [File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: repay() + false-return loanToken] Can an unprivileged borrower under a market whose loanToken returns false on transferFrom call repay(market, units, onBehalf, address(0), '') so that SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), units) (line 520) reverts with TransferFromReturnedFalse after position[id][onBehalf].debt and marketState[id].withdrawable have been updated (lines 508-509) but the whole tx reverts, violating the liveness invariant that a borrower must always be able to repay debt, causing scoped impact: permanent debt repayment freeze if loanToken's transferFrom returns false, making positions permanently liquidatable? [File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: claimSettlementFee() + false-return token] Can an unprivileged feeClaimer under a market whose loanToken returns false on transfer call claimSettlementFee(token, amount, receiver) so that SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(token, receiver, amount) (line 309) reverts with TransferReturnedFalse after claimableSettlementFee[token] has been decremented (line 307) but the whole tx reverts, violating the liveness invariant that accrued settlement fees must always be claimable, causing scoped impact: permanent settlement fee freeze if loanToken's transfer returns false? [File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: take() + IBuyCallback.onBuy() reverts] Can an unprivileged maker under a buy offer with a buyerCallback that always reverts cause take() to always revert at the require(IBuyCallback(buyerCallback).onBuy(...) == CALLBACK_SUCCESS, WrongBuyCallbackReturnValue()) check (lines 447-452) after all position state, consumed, totalUnits, and claimableSettlementFee have been updated (lines 408-418) but the whole tx reverts, violating the liveness invariant that a valid offer with a valid ratifier must be takeable, causing scoped impact: permanent DoS on any buy offer whose callback contract is broken or malicious? [File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: take() + IBuyCallback returns wrong bytes32] Can an unprivileged maker under a buy offer with a buyerCallback that returns a non-CALLBACK_SUCCESS bytes32 cause take() to revert with WrongBuyCallbackReturnValue (lines 447-452) after all accounting state has been updated, violating the liveness invariant that a valid offer must be takeable, causing scoped impact: permanent DoS on any buy offer whose callback returns an incorrect magic value, even if the callback otherwise executes correctly? [File: 'src/Midnight.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: take() + ERC777 loanToken + tokensToSend hook reenters take()] Can an unprivileged taker under a market whose loanToken is ERC777 trigger a tokensToSend hook during safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) (line 455) that reenters take() with the same seller, so that the seller's debt is increased a second time before the outer take()'s health check at line 476, violating the invariant that the seller's debt after take() must pass isHealthy() with the collateral at the time of the outer call, causing scoped impact: seller's position may be left unhealthy if the reentrant take() increases debt beyond the collateral threshold?
